artifacts.content:
From: jordan.rivera15@gmail.com
To: elenarivera.az@gmail.com
Subject: Dairy + spicy food stuff (so I don’t mess up work again)

Hey Mom,

I was packing my lunch for tomorrow and was just thinking about all the times my stomach has betrayed me this past year lol, so I wanted to write it down so we’re on the same page.

First, I’m still low‑key traumatized from that giant milkshake on my dinner break at Fry’s on W Peoria. Remember March 2024 when I chugged it, got those awful cramps and diarrhea, and had to leave my shift like 2 hours early? Dr. Alvarez at Banner Thunderbird Medical Center basically told us I need to stay away from heavy cream stuff at school and work. So no more giant shakes or super creamy pasta before shifts or youth events.

Then there was early September 2023 after the carne asada at Abuela Rosa’s apartment in Peoria. All that queso and tres leches wrecked me so bad you ended up taking me to Abrazo Arrowhead Campus urgent care late that night. The doctor there said it was a bad lactose reaction and I should seriously limit big dairy portions. I’m fine with a little cheese, just not mountains of it.

And I definitely learned my lesson with spicy food too. In May 2024, that habanero burrito during my 30‑minute dinner break at Fry’s made me lightheaded and almost puke. I hid in the bathroom an extra 15 minutes, and my manager Luis wrote me up for it. I get why you keep telling me not to eat super spicy stuff before work.

So if you’re grabbing food for me before a shift or a youth thing, can we stick to non‑dairy or light cheese and not‑crazy‑spicy? Water or juice instead of milk is fine.

Love,
Jordan

metadata.subject: Dairy + spicy food stuff (so I don’t mess up work again)

artifacts.content:
Title: Food / allergy rules for myself

1. Dairy
- Remember the disaster milkshake from my Fry’s dinner break in March 2024. I was at the Fry’s Food & Drug on W Peoria Ave, downed that huge shake, then ended up with cramps/diarrhea and had to leave my shift 2 hours early. Dr. Alvarez at Banner Thunderbird Medical Center told Mom and me I should stay away from heavy cream‑based stuff at school/work.
- Also think about early September 2023 at Grandma Rosa Delgado’s apartment in Peoria. Carne asada + way too much queso + tres leches = late‑night trip to Abrazo Arrowhead Campus urgent care with Mom. Doctor there said “bad lactose reaction” and to seriously limit big dairy portions.
- Action: small amounts of cheese/yogurt = ok. Avoid big milkshakes, Alfredo, huge cheesy casseroles, giant tres leches servings, especially before shifts or long events.

2. Spicy food
- May 2024: super spicy habanero burrito during my 30‑minute dinner break at Fry’s on W Peoria. Got lightheaded and almost threw up, hid in the bathroom an extra 15 minutes. Manager Luis wrote me up for going over break.
- Action: no “how hot can you go” challenges before work or youth stuff. Mild salsa only.

3. Allergies
- April 2024: Cactus View High School club picnic. Pollen was crazy, my sneezing/runny nose got so bad some teachers thought it was COVID and sent me home. School nurse Ms. Patel wrote a note about avoiding outdoor lunch on high‑pollen days.
- Action: check pollen count, take cetirizine in the morning, ask to sit inside/away from grass when it’s bad.

Bottom line: don’t be dumb with dairy, spice, or pollen, especially on work days.

Group chat: "CVHS Hoopers" (Me, Diego, Marcus)

7:22 PM
Me: nurse Ms. Patel even wrote a note in April 2024 saying I should avoid outdoor lunch on super high‑pollen days. so if we hoop and it’s bad, I’m popping a cetirizine first thing.

7:23 PM
Marcus: old man allergies 😂 bring tissues grandpa

7:25 PM

7:26 PM
Diego: WAIT what happened

7:28 PM
Me: March 2024, Fry’s on W Peoria. Huge milkshake on my dinner break, then my stomach exploded. had to leave my shift like 2 hrs early. Mom dragged me to Dr. Alvarez at Banner Thunderbird and he was like “maybe don’t bathe your organs in dairy”

7:30 PM
Marcus: 💀💀💀

7:32 PM
Me: and don’t forget the habanero burrito in May at Fry’s. 30‑min break turned into 45 cuz I was dying in the bathroom. manager Luis wrote me up. I’m officially retired from spicy dares.

7:34 PM
Diego: ok so no pollen, no milkshakes, no lava burritos. got it. see u 4pm if the air doesn’t try to kill us

From: jordan.rivera15@gmail.com
To: rosa.delgado.peoria@example.com
Subject: Sunday comida ideas

Hola Abuela,

Mom said we’re doing carne asada at your apartment in Peoria again this Sunday, so I wanted to email you before you go shopping.

Can we maybe do a few more non‑dairy options this time? I still remember early September 2023 when we had that huge carne asada at your place with all the queso and the giant slices of tres leches. It was delicious, but my stomach did not agree. Mom ended up taking me to the Abrazo Arrowhead Campus urgent care late that night, and the doctor there said it was a bad lactose reaction and that I should seriously limit big dairy portions.

I’m not saying no cheese at all (I’d cry if you took away the quesadillas completely 😅). I just probably shouldn’t pile on mountains of queso or eat half a tray of tres leches by myself. Smaller portions are fine, and I’m totally happy with more carne, rice, beans, and maybe some fruit or salad.

Also, I’m trying to be careful with super spicy stuff now. In May 2024 at my Fry’s job on W Peoria Ave, I ate this crazy habanero burrito during my 30‑minute dinner break, got lightheaded and almost threw up, hid in the bathroom an extra 15 minutes, and my manager Luis actually wrote me up for taking too long. So I’m sticking to mild salsa most of the time.

I’ll bring my reusable water bottle like always, so don’t worry about special drinks. Water and maybe some juice are perfect. I just don’t want to spend another night in urgent care.

Love you,
Jordan

Title: Youth night + allergy / food plan

Date: Friday, 2024-04-19
Time: 5:30 PM – 9:00 PM
Location: Community Center (near Cactus View HS)

Notes to self:

- Check pollen count in the morning. If it’s high like it was during that Cactus View High School club picnic earlier in April 2024, take cetirizine with breakfast. I don’t want a repeat of the sneezing/runny nose disaster where teachers thought I had COVID and sent me home. If they set up dinner tables outside on the grass, ask if I can sit inside or at least away from trees.

- Food: NO giant dairy bombs. Remember March 2024 at Fry’s Food & Drug on W Peoria Ave when I slammed that huge milkshake on my dinner break, got horrible cramps/diarrhea, and had to leave my shift 2 hours early. Dr. Alvarez at Banner Thunderbird Medical Center told Mom and me to avoid heavy cream‑based stuff at school/work.

- Also think about early September 2023 after the carne asada at Abuela Rosa’s apartment in Peoria. Too much queso + tres leches = late‑night trip to Abrazo Arrowhead Campus urgent care with Mom. Doctor said it was a bad lactose reaction and to seriously limit big dairy portions.

- Spicy stuff: skip any “super hot” wings or mystery chili. In May 2024 at Fry’s, that habanero burrito during my 30‑minute dinner break made me lightheaded, almost puking, and I hid in the bathroom an extra 15 minutes. Manager Luis wrote me up. Not worth it.

Goal: enjoy games, hang with people, come home without my stomach or allergies exploding.

From: jordan.rivera15@gmail.com
To: ms.lopez@cactusviewhs.edu
Subject: Allergies + outdoor activities

Hi Ms. Lopez,

I wanted to check in about some of the outdoor things coming up since my allergies and stomach stuff have been acting up at random times.

During spring allergy season this year, my symptoms got pretty intense. At the club picnic in April 2024 at Cactus View High School, my sneezing and runny nose got so bad that a couple of teachers thought I might have COVID and I was sent home early. After that, the school nurse, Ms. Patel, wrote a note saying I should avoid outdoor lunch events on really high‑pollen days when possible. I’ve been taking cetirizine in the mornings when the pollen count is high, which helps.

I’m also trying to be more careful about food around school activities. In March 2024, during my dinner break at the Fry’s Food & Drug on W Peoria Ave where I work as a cashier, I had a huge milkshake and ended up with bad stomach cramps and diarrhea. I had to leave my shift 2 hours early, and when my mom took me to see Dr. Alvarez at Banner Thunderbird Medical Center, he told us I should stay away from heavy cream‑based foods at school and work.

That wasn’t the first time dairy hit me hard. Back in early September 2023 after a family carne asada at my grandma Rosa Delgado’s apartment in Peoria, I got super bloated and nauseous from all the queso and tres leches. Mom took me to the Abrazo Arrowhead Campus urgent care, and they called it a bad lactose reaction and told me to seriously limit big dairy portions.

If any school events involve eating outside during peak pollen, or big dairy‑heavy meals, is it okay if I choose a different spot to sit or pick lighter options? I’m totally fine participating otherwise.

Thank you,
Jordan Rivera
10th grade, Cactus View High School
